channel.title: HEIR – HEIR: Homomorphic Encryption Intermediate Representation
channel.description: Recent content in HEIR: Homomorphic Encryption Intermediate Representation on HEIR

item.title: Blog: HEIR meeting 2026-04-23
item.description: Here are the notes and video recording from the HEIR meeting on 2026-04-23.

item.description: Here are the notes and video recording from the HEIR meeting on 2025-10-30.

item.title: Blog: HEIR meeting 2025-02-20
item.description: Here are the notes and video recording from the HEIR meeting on 2025-02-20.

item.title: Blog: HEIR as a Platform for Research (and new YouTube Channel)
item.description:
On 2024-12-05, the HEIR team presented a talk aimed at giving researchers a high-level overview of the HEIR project, brainstorming where new research might be needed, and discussing how the HEIR team could help with research projects.

The video recording of this talk is on YouTube, and this is the first video posted on the new HEIR YouTube channel.

item.title: Blog: HEIR meeting 2024-07-18 (mod_arith dialect)
item.description: Here are the notes and video recording from the HEIR meeting on 2024-07-18. This special discussion was focused on the modular arithmetic dialect.

item.title: Blog: HEIR meeting 2023-10-24
item.description:
Here are the notes and video recording from the HEIR meeting on 2023-10-24.

Also of note: our talk at FHE.org was last Thursday (recording), and the similar talk at the LLVM developers meeting talk was the previous Wednesday, though the video has not yet been posted for that.

item.title: Blog: HEIR meeting 2023-09-26
item.description:
Here are the notes and video recording from the HEIR meeting on 2023-09-26.

Next meeting (Oct 10) is canceled for the MLIR workshop, and I’ll post an update from that workshop on this blog, hopefully also with a video of the talk that Alex Viand and I will be giving at the LLVM developers meeting.

item.title: Blog: HEIR meeting 2023-08-01
item.description:
The fourth HEIR meeting was 2023-08-01 (notes and video recording).

We’re also starting on the Poly and Secret dialects in #74 (poly) and #78 (secret), so please join in the fun!
